nextWorkspace: Flagship Foundry — UI/UX Implementation and Verification

workOrders.title: Operator task corpus and quick-action inventory
constraints: Start from existing operator surfaces; do not assume a greenfield product shell.
Identify top 10 operator decisions and top 10 quick actions.
Separate high-frequency triage tasks from deep architecture work.
acceptanceCriteria: Top operator goals are documented as jobs-to-be-done.
Quick actions are ranked by urgency and frequency.
At least one drill-down chain from ecosystem to evidence is explicitly defined.
verificationPlan: Manual review of task corpus completeness
Cross-check with architecture packet and plan

workOrders.title: Object model and state-authority matrix
constraints: No new source of truth.
Keep declared artifacts separate from observed runtime state.
Align with current workspace target, context cart, visual artifact, planning board, and session board artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Every first-class object has an owner and authority defined.
Declared and observed artifacts are not conflated.
Primary and secondary surfaces are explicit.

workOrders.title: Cockpit shell screen map and navigation contract
constraints: Top bar, left rail, center canvas, right inspector, bottom utility are the baseline desktop shell.
Do not introduce hidden navigation as the primary path.
Favor object-based navigation over menu-based navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop shell shows stable spatial frame.
Map/Board/Timeline/Diff are defined as peer views.
Inspector and utility drawer are explicit.

workOrders.title: ADR set for canonical cockpit and projection surfaces
constraints: Cursor Companion remains the write-capable first surface.
Foundry app / graph viewer remain read/projection surfaces.
Execution still flows through policy gateway and existing run control.
acceptanceCriteria: Canonical write surface is explicit.
Projection surfaces are read-only by default.
State authority separation is locked by ADR.

workOrders.title: ADR set for drill-down chain and motion/accessibility baseline
constraints: Drill-down must move from ecosystem → subsystem → module → pseudocode → diff → work order → run → evidence.
Toggle controls are for immediate binary settings, not consequential workflow actions.
Reduced-motion and keyboard semantics must be part of the baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: A stable drill-down chain is defined.
Motion categories and toggle rules are explicit.
Accessibility baseline is listed as a release gate.

workOrders.title: Schema and view-registry extensions for UI/UX cockpit
constraints: Extend the existing operator schema family; do not create duplicate artifact classes.
Every new artifact must identify whether it is declared or observed.
Saved layouts must be user-scoped and reversible.
acceptanceCriteria: All new schemas validate.
View registry supports peer views and drill-down targets.
Quick actions and saved layouts are represented as typed artifacts.

workOrders.title: Cockpit shell, navigation rails, panel manager, and saved layouts
constraints: Preserve existing run controls while re-composing shell layout.
Do not remove current plan/import/gap analysis flows.
Shell must support dock/undock/hide/show inspector and utility panels.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop shell exposes top bar, left rail, center canvas, right inspector, and bottom utility drawer.
Panels can be shown/hidden without losing context.
At least Architect, Planner, Operator, and Investigator saved layouts exist.

workOrders.title: Graph interaction upgrades and map-centric navigation
constraints: Graph remains navigation and projection, not a second editor.
Support fit-to-selection, reset view, minimap, zoom, pan, focus path, and compare selection.
Overlay run and evidence state without mutating declared topology.
acceptanceCriteria: Operators can focus, zoom, compare, and reset quickly.
Run/evidence overlays are distinct from declared graph structure.
Graph nodes expose drill-down destinations.

workOrders.title: Inspector and drill-down chain for pseudocode, diff, work order, run, and evidence
constraints: Use a single inspector pattern instead of scattered modal dialogs.
Preserve artifact authority and evidence provenance in drill-down views.
Diff view must explain what changed, what was added/removed, and why.
acceptanceCriteria: Operators can reach pseudocode, diff, work orders, runs, and evidence from a selected node/module.
Breadcrumbs reflect the drill-down path.
Inspector always shows valid actions and current state.
verificationPlan: manual path trace: node → pseudocode → diff → work order → run → evidence

workOrders.title: Command palette, quick actions, and chat-driven orchestration affordances
constraints: Quick actions must expose the top-ranked operator actions with minimal navigation.
Chat suggestions may recommend agents and pipelines but must still honor approval boundaries.
Command palette is global; it cannot bypass policy or state authority.
acceptanceCriteria: Create feature, compile pipeline, inspect evidence, open workspace target, and switch layout are reachable from a command palette.
Quick actions are visible in contextually appropriate places.
Chat suggestions can pre-fill a work-order draft or pipeline recommendation without auto-executing.

workOrders.title: Plan board and session board convergence
constraints: Plan state, approval posture, and session state must remain distinguishable but linked.
Show blocked conditions, verifier posture, evidence refs, and continuity refs together when relevant.
Do not conflate draft plan status with observed run progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Operator can move from plan dependency to live session view without context loss.
Blocked conditions and approval posture are visible.
Evidence links appear on the same object path.

workOrders.title: Design tokens, motion system, toggles, and feedback states
constraints: Toggle controls are only for immediate binary settings such as live updates, telemetry overlay, or follow selection.
Compile, Approve, Launch, Stop remain explicit action controls.
Reduced-motion behavior must exist from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: Motion clarifies state changes and navigation relationships.
Toggle states are visually obvious and semantically correct.
Loading, empty, success, warning, and error states are defined.

workOrders.title: Mobile adaptation for iOS and Android triage surfaces
constraints: Do not force full desktop parity on mobile.
Mobile focuses on triage, approvals, alerts, search, and lightweight evidence review.
Graph authoring remains desktop-first.
acceptanceCriteria: A clear capability split exists between desktop and mobile.
iOS and Android navigation models are explicit.
Approval/monitoring tasks are prioritized.

workOrders.title: Accessibility, performance, and scenario verification
constraints: Verify scenario outcomes, not just component existence.
Include keyboard/focus, reduced-motion, graph performance, and safe launch/recovery.
Do not merge observed evidence into declared truth without annotation.
acceptanceCriteria: Can the operator find the active run in under 10 seconds?
Can the operator explain why a work order is blocked?
Can the operator get from topology to pseudocode diff in 3 clicks?
Can the operator recover from a failed launch without losing context?

workOrders.title: Release handoff, telemetry, and learning loop
constraints: Every release claim must reference evidence.
Emit telemetry and learning hooks for the next improvement cycle.
Preserve import/export compatibility for downstream workspaces.
acceptanceCriteria: Bundle includes plan, architecture, ADRs, work orders, evidence, and import manifest.
Next workspace and approval steps are explicit.
Learning loop and telemetry hooks are documented.
